text.heading: ASSESSMENT OF DR. XXX'S PUBLICATIONS FOR THE GRADE OF ASSOCIATE PROFESSOR IN THE DEPARTMENT OF XXX, FACULTY OF XXX, XXX UNIVERSITY, XXX
text.p:
Thank you for your e-mail of 8th February, 2011 signifying your willingness to assist our Appointments and Promotions [Academic] Committee in assessing the publications of Dr.
to the grade of Associate Professor in the Department of , Faculty Of , University,
Towards this end, please find enclosed the following documents for assessment:
a) Curriculum Vitae including list of publications
b) Best ten [10] Academic Publications ranked in order of priority by the candidate and
c) Other publications of the candidate.

The Appointments and Promotion [Academic] Committee would be greatly assisted if you would give a detailed assessment of each of the publications. The quality of each publication should be rated, and in addition, please sum up your assessment(s) and quantify them since our University Regulations require that points be awarded for publications. In the assessment of candidate, each of the publications is to be scored upon a maximum of 10 points. The assessor is then requested to sum up all the points a candidate has obtained upon the total maximum points obtainable; for example, a candidate with 15 publications will be assessed upon maximum obtainable point of 150.
Finally, the assessor is required to convert this o a percentage score. Please note that an Associate Professor, in order to be appointable, must obtain a percentage score of at least 65%.

You are therefore required to please look at the publications of the applicant in their totality and decide whether these constitute sufficient basis for the appointment to the post of Associate Professor and indicate this by a score of the candidate's publications out of a total maximum obtainable score of 100 percent. It is important to emphasize that the Appointments and Promotions (Academic) Committee will insist on your scoring and will not be able to proceed with the candidate unless scores are awarded.

boldface.italics: When you will have finished with the publications, kindly inform Prof. . . , Ag. Vice-Chancellor, University through her e-mail address or on telephone number and the Courier Service (DHL) will be directed to pick the publications and the report from your end for onward delivery to University.

text.p: For further information kindly contact the Deputy Registrar, Academic Staff Establishment Division, Mrs. . . on these numbers ; . OR Principal Executive Officer I, Mrs. . . on .
